free_text: Neutron Total Cross Sections of 232Th, 237Np, 235,238U,
and 239Pu from 3 to 230 MeV
The Weapons Neutron Research Facility
of the Los Alamos Neutron Science Center.
The neutron detector consisted of a 10.2-cm-
diameter x 15.2-cm-thick NE-110 scintillator coupled
to the front face of an RCA 8854 photomultiplier tube.
All measurements were performed in good
geometry on a 31.78-m flight path whose length was
determined from observation of resonances in neutron
transmission through a graphite sample. Total cross
sections were determined from the ratio of the number
of neutrons transmitted T through a sample N to the
number transmitted without a sample N0: T = N/N0 =
exp(-n*sigma), where sigma = neutron total cross
section(b), n = number of atoms per barn in the sample.
After correcting all spectra for dead-time losses, a
normalized background spectrum was subtracted from
both sample-in and sample-out data. A small residual
time-uncorrelated background, typically less than 1%,
of the counts in each energy bin was also subtracted.
Data for individual time channels used the difference
between neutron detection and the prompt-gamma-ray
signal to determine the neutron energy using
relativistic equations. The time-of-flight results
were combined into bins of approximately 2.5% or 5%
energy resolution and converted to total cross
sections as a function of neutron energy.
The uncertainty in the total cross section dsigma was
then calculated for each energy bin from dsigma =
dT/(n*T) .
Contributions from the statistical
uncertainties in N and N0 as well as background error
and monitor uncertainty were included in the usual way.
Based on our analysis of relative
fluctuations in system counting rates for the same
samples over multiple data sets, the well determined
value of n (atoms/barn) , and the small backgrounds,
we estimate the systematic uncertainties to be
between 0.5-0.7%.
Dead-time losses.
Approved by P.W.Lisowski on 02/23/2024.
